steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sint' — 'sunt' is indicative

hints.sequor, sequi, secutus sum: These are the parts of 'sequi' (to follow) — not 'esse'
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand) — different from 'esse'
hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — not 'esse'

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'res' — 'rerum' is the genitive plural
hints.Accusative: Accusative plural would be 'res' — 'rerum' is genitive plural
hints.Dative: Dative plural would be 'rebus' — 'rerum' is the genitive plural

hints.1st: 1st declension genitive plural ends in -arum — 'res, rei' is 5th declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension genitive plural ends in -um/-ium — 'rerum' is 5th declension
hints.4th: 4th declension genitive plural ends in -uum — 'rerum' is 5th declension

hints.Accusative: Neuter plural nominative and accusative are identical — but as subject of 'sunt' this is nominative
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural would be 'principiis' — 'principia' is nominative plural
hints.Vocative: Cicero is not addressing the beginnings — 'principia' is the subject

hints.1st: 1st declension nominative plural ends in -ae — 'principium, principii' is 2nd declension neuter
hints.3rd: 3rd declension neuter plural would end in -a too, but 'principium' is 2nd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'principium' is 2nd declension neuter

translation: The beginnings of all things are small.
